If you’ve been dreaming of a peaceful place with mountain views, fresh air, and friendly neighbours — this is it.

With three bedrooms and two bathrooms, including a main en-suite, this home is designed to meet all your accommodation needs. Sundew Villas is a secure, gated estate offering fantastic amenities, including a sparkling communal pool, a dog park, a children's play area, and scenic walking paths. The estate is within walking distance of the beach and conveniently close to Curro School, Whale Coast Mall, family restaurants, hospitals, wine farms, and many other amenities.

This property is perfect as an investment, a first-time buyer's dream, or a cozy retreat for couples looking to downscale. Don’t miss out on this gem. Contact us today to arrange a private viewing of our Exclusive Sole Mandate.

Two separate garages, both with motorised doors, one of which has larger access for a caravan/boat. The house has an inverter and two solar panels. Pre paid electricity. The property is fenced on all sides with a motorised access gate. Lots of space on this well presented property with attractive garden to the front and rear, in close proximity to the coast with guaranteed views to the south. Viewing recommended.

descriptionHeader: An income producing gem in the quiet lower avenues
description:
Exclusive Mandate - Thoughtfully designed with income producing flatlet/s.   This charming clinker brick home, with a large indoor fireplace and pool is ready to be snapped up. It's lovely situation and quiet area just adds to all its clever features.

Fabulous expansive views from the large deck with shade trees, pool to cool off in and offering a good deal of privacy.  Walled all round. Plenty of on site parking with remote controlled entrance gate.

The house can be described as a 3 bedroomed house, all ensuite with an office OR a 2 bedroomed, with an office plus a beautiful spacious studio flatlet with magnificent views over vineyards, farmlands and mountains in the distance.

Entering the house from the deck is the dining area which leads into a comfy lounge with the large fireplace for those wintery nights.

The kitchen is modern and well designed with hob and built in oven, plenty of cupboards, small laundry area and bar style breakfast or supper nook - all flows neatly from one to the other room.

All bedrooms, apart from being en suite, have ceiling fans, enough BIC and the windows are burglar barred.

Step out onto the neatly paved back area, you have 2 extra large double garages and another building, which with minimum effort, could be converted into another studio flatlet.

Behind this to be converted flatlet is the pool, protected and most inviting.

This is a good investment as there is virtually no maintenance, roof is green chromadek, there is a security camera covering the whole front of the house, and you have interesting options which need to be seen to be understood!! 

Botriver is close to Hermanus, Kleinmond and Caledon.  We enjoy a Mediterranean climate.  We have an excellent patrolling security company and neighborhood watch group.  Crime is virtually non existant.  We live here and love it.  The people are friendly.  Botriver is becoming popular being only just over 1 hour to Cape Town, still affordable and yet a country dorpie!

Give me a call to view this really good and potentially great property.  My client is a serious seller so we are looking for serious buyers.

description:

Recently renovated and move-in ready, the house offers light-filled interiors with large windows that frame garden and river views. The versatile layout allows for **3 to 4 bedrooms**, making it ideal for families, creatives, or anyone seeking space to work from home. Screed and tiled floors add to the home’s understated, contemporary character, while the open-plan living and dining areas create an easy flow for everyday living and entertaining.

The kitchen is practical and well-positioned, with connections for a dishwasher and washing machine, and direct access to a storeroom or pantry that could easily be converted into a laundry or additional utility space. An enclosed back stoep with Trellidor security provides a sheltered entertainment area that works beautifully year-round.

A separate **double garage/workshop with its own toilet and basin** opens up exciting possibilities — whether as a garden flat, studio, or work-from-home space.

Outside, the established, water-wise garden is shaded by mature trees and designed for low maintenance living. The covered braai area and firepit overlook the river, offering a truly special setting for relaxed evenings with family and friends. Secure fencing ensures peace of mind for children and pets, while gated river access leads to a private stretch of fynbos across the water, alive with birdlife and small wildlife.

This is not just a home — it’s a lifestyle of space, privacy, and connection with nature, all set on a generous **2,379 m² erf**.

**Why contact me?**  
I live and work in this community and understand both the value of riverfront properties and the nuances that make them special. I offer honest advice, local insight, and personal service — ensuring you fully understand the opportunities this property presents, now and into the future. If you’re looking for more than just a transaction, and want guidance you can trust, I’m here to help.

descriptionHeader: House for sale with both sea and mountain view in betty’s bay
description:
This house, built and with first occupancy in 2003 is situated above Clarence Drive at the foothills of the Kogelberg Mountains, which embrace the Kogelberg Biosphere Reserve. It encompasses a large open plan dining room and lounge area. The kitchen flowing from here leads onto a covered patio with a large "Jet Master" type double-door de luxe built-in braai. The house further comprises of 3 bedrooms, with the main being an en-suite with a walk-through double-sided built-in closet leading to a fully tiled bathroom consisting of bath, basin, and shower. There are also built-in window boxes leading from the basin wall around the bath, ending at the shower wall. The other 2 bedrooms share a fully tiled family bathroom with a shower, bath, and basin. The TV room has been temporarily enclosed and currently serves as a third bedroom. It can, however, easily be reverted back to its original purpose. 

Further, leading from the en-suite sliding door, you enter the vast atrium. This area has an exit door to the side garden space bordering the en-suite bedroom window.  It also leads to a large laundry room in the far corner with a door leading to the garage. This can also be converted into another bedroom, as there is a toilet and basin in the garage space that the interleading door opens to. On the far side is a sliding door that takes you to the garage. The garage is 137.70m2 in extent with a storeroom against the far wall.  It was originally used as a workshop and has a car pit for mechanical vehicle maintenance. The garage can further accommodate up to 6 cars, if required. However, due to its size thereof it can easily be converted into either a complete flatlet with rental and/or Airbnb probability or serve as a living space for an adolescent or aged parents with close family ties. This house has huge potential for a buyer with a view to expanding horizons and is a must-see.
Contact me to arrange a viewing!

Ample parking available — ideal for stopping in off the N2 or meeting friends for a relaxed meal in the garden.
Licensed for on-consumption alcohol service, the Coffee Shop also offers a small but carefully curated retail section that features:

•	Handmade artisanal items (ceramics, toys, candles, maps) – Not included in the sale
•	The sale includes a valid on-consumption liquor license, allowing alcohol service with meals.

descriptionHeader: No. 4 Seaview Drive ~ Betty's Bay ~ Overberg
description:
Exclusive Sole Mandate 

This exceptional 1 870sqm plot on Sea View Drive, Betty’s Bay offers a rare opportunity to build your dream home in one of the Overbergs most sought-after coastal locations.
Elevated and well-positioned, the stand enjoys sweeping mountain backdrops and beautiful sea views, creating a setting that is both tranquil and inspiring.
Surrounded by natural fynbos and quality homes, the property offers generous space for a spacious residence while still preserving privacy and a strong connection to nature.
Ideal for buyers seeking space, views and long-term investment value, this stand combines natural beauty with excellent building potential in a prime location.

Betty’s Bay with its perfect sandy beaches, tranquil lagoons and quiet trails through fynbos, is not only home to the Stony Point African Penguin colony and Harold Porter National Botanical Gardens, but also hardy residents who fight continually to keep their natural environment as pure and unspoilt as possible.
The town was named after Betty Youlden, the daughter of the property developer who, together with Harold Porter and Jack Clarence, bought up farms in the area in the 1930’s and formed a consortium that laid out the settlements of Betty’s Bay, Rooiels and Pringle Bay. This area wasn’t always as accessible as it is today and Jack Clarence was mainly responsible for replacing the rough footpath between Gordon’s Bay and Rooiels with a proper road built by Italian prisoners of war during World War II. Today Clarence Drive must be one of the most breathtaking drives in South Africa with its towering mountains on one side, the sparkling ocean on the other and majestic views across False Bay to the Cape Peninsula.
Things not to miss!

•	Sandboarding down the Silversands dunes.
•	Wander through spectacular cultivated and natural displays of coastal fynbos in the Harold Porter National Botanical Garden.
•	Volunteer to be an Oystercatcher nanny during the summer holiday season.
•	View one of only two land-based penguin colonies at Stony Point.
•	Visit Master Chocolatier, Gaspard Bossut, who practices his trade under the name Gaboli Chocolates from his home and shop in Delport Road

descriptionHeader: Light and Modern 3 Bedroom House in Pringle Bay for Sale
description:
DUAL MANDATE

A beautiful cottage, with modern finishes and a cozy, welcoming atmosphere—perfect for starting your Pringle Bay property journey.

Not sure what to expect? Pringle Bay is a charming village on the Cape Whale Coast, where morning beach strolls lead to friendly chats with locals, where coffee at a deli turns into a catch-up, and where mountain trails wind through nature, perfect for a morning jog or a peaceful walk. It’s a place to be carefree and happy, surrounded by a warm and supportive community.

But let’s talk about this wonderful home! Offering three bedrooms, two bathrooms (both with showers), an office space, and an open-plan kitchen and lounge, this property is designed for both comfort and practicality. And then there’s the highlight: the veranda, where you’ll likely spend most of your time soaking in breathtaking mountain views. Picture this—layers of mountains stretching into the distance, incredible sunsets, sugarbirds flitting past, the soothing hum of crickets, and a canopy of stars above (thanks to the absence of streetlights).

Inside, a quality wood-burning fireplace and gas geyser keep things warm and cozy during the cooler months, while large sliding doors and big windows flood the space with natural light. Built on a solid, slightly elevated foundation, the home is designed to prevent rising damp. Plus, all inner walls are non-load-bearing, meaning you can easily reconfigure the space to suit your needs.

The layout is both practical and inviting:

The entrance veranda leads into the open-plan living area with a fireplace and kitchen.

A staircase takes you up to a spacious loft-style room with stunning views of the mountains, village, and even distant sea glimpses.

Off the kitchen, a short hallway leads to the main en-suite bedroom.

Also extending from the kitchen are the office space and third bedroom, with a storage area at the far end housing the solar system—because yes, this home is completely off the grid!

The low-maintenance garden is a delight, with a water tank and irrigation system already in place for easy upkeep. There’s also ample yard space to add a tandem garage if desired, and the property is fully fenced for privacy.

Beyond this beautiful home, Pringle Bay itself is an experience. The village is alive with creativity, from its colourful shops in the CBD to intimate house dinners and live music performances at the iconic Hangklip Hotel. Thursdays bring the Hangklip Athletics Club’s evening runs and walks, while the Pringle Tingle cold-water swimmers embrace the refreshing ocean. The town’s quiz nights spark friendly competition, and the full moon bicycle rides offer a magical way to explore the village under the stars. Pringle Bay also celebrates its artistic spirit through regular exhibitions showcasing the work of talented local artists.

Let's do this. Book your viewing today. Looking forward to welcoming you home!

descriptionHeader: Step into history – coffee and gift boutique in riviersonderend.
description:
This remarkable property offers a rare opportunity to own a fully operational business in one of Riviersonderend’s most historic buildings. Zoned Business Zone 2 and sold as a running concern, the property forms an integral part of the town’s heritage. 

Although detailed records are limited, it is widely believed that the building was constructed in 1931 by Mr. Diedericks, making it one of the oldest structures in Riviersonderend. Originally built as a mill serving the local farming community, its legacy continues to define its timeless character today.

In 1983, it became the property of Jos van Eeden, whose father served as the local meulenaar (miller). Jos added a balcony and stoep, and the building later operated as a bakery run by Mrs. Rina Eksteen, and afterward as a butchery. The owner of the butchery added a cold room adjacent to the kitchen, which is currently used as a storeroom but could easily be converted back into a functioning cold room with the right equipment.

The current owners bought in 2014 and have preserved this character while enhancing the space to suit a modern café and retail environment. Today, the property offers a unique experience: guests can enjoy a sit-down meal indoors, on the stoep, or in the beautiful garden, which is a major attraction for families and travellers alike. The garden seating is pet-friendly, and there is a charming play park for children—complete with a green tractor—making it a favourite stop for families.

Unlike most local spots that focus on fast food and coffee-to-go, Padloper Café invites you to slow down and savour the moment. Here, time stands still—you’ll be welcomed by friendly staff and treated to meals prepared on-site using fresh, locally sourced ingredients. The menu caters to a variety of tastes, ensuring a high-quality dining experience. Alcoholic beverages are available under an on-consumption liquor license.

There is also an open area on the top floor, currently used as an office, accessible via an outside staircase. If an internal staircase were installed, this space could be repurposed for almost anything—such as an art gallery, art classes, wine tasting, or an extension of the craft and gift section of the coffee shop. This top floor boasts a large balcony with breathtaking, uninterrupted mountain views.
Underneath the coffee shop is a cool storage area, perfect for storing wine.

The property sits on a 945 m² stand and is sold fully equipped, including all appliances and furniture—ready for you to step in and continue its story.

Unique properties like this rarely come to market. Be the lucky new owner of a true piece of Riviersonderend history.

The total stand size of the current property is 2,479 m². The owner is subdividing it into two separate stands. The front stand, where the coffee shop is located, will be 945 m². The back stand, which includes a garage, will be 1,521 m² and will be rezoned to residential once the subdivision is finalized. This back stand is also for sale for:  R920 000

Betty's Bay is a scenic seaside village in South Africa's Western Cape, nestled between the Kogelberg Mountains and the Atlantic Ocean on the R44 scenic drive. Exclusive Sole Mandate – Nestled in a lovely part of Sandbaai, this charming home has had a bit of a refresh and is looking wonderfully neat and welcoming.

With three comfortable bedrooms and two bathrooms, plus built-in cupboards throughout, it’s a great fit for families or anyone looking for the perfect holiday home.

You’ll love the enclosed braai room and spacious patio—perfect for relaxed entertaining and enjoying the outdoors. The main bedroom also opens onto its own private patio, adding an extra touch of tranquility.

The kitchen is a good size with plenty of cupboard space, and there’s a handy separate storage area inside the house that could easily be used as a laundry.

A generous double garage sits in the back garden, offering secure parking for two vehicles.

Get in touch to arrange a viewing—we’d love to show you around!

descriptionHeader: 30 Korperasie – A Central Gansbaai Gem With Mountain Views
description:
This exceptional, upgraded home in the heart of Gansbaai offers an ideal blend of comfort, luxury, and lock-up-and-go convenience. With 3 bedrooms, 2.5 bathrooms, a double garage, and an open plan entertainment-focused layout, this property is move-in ready - sold fully furnished, including appliances, cutlery, crockery, bed linen, and towels. Simply bring your clothes.
Key Features:
Modern Living & Entertainment
•	Spacious braai room with granite countertops leading directly from the double garage.
•	Solar-heated swimming pool (two-pump system) with surrounding paving.
•	Tiled garage floor allows the entertainment space to extend when hosting larger gatherings.
Quality Upgrades Throughout
•	All gates and burglar bars are galvanized and powder-coated to resist coastal rust.
•	Trelli Doors, electric fencing, and a comprehensive alarm system with 21 beams offer top-tier security.
•	Three aluminium-and-glass wind enclosures with stainless-steel balustrades provide shelter from the South Easter while maintaining light and flow.
•	Upgraded kitchen with scullery and Granite tops and appliances.
•	Air-conditioning Compressors neatly positioned at the back, complete with water drainage systems.
•	300-litre solar geyser for energy-efficient hot water.
•	Automated 7.5 kVA generator system ensures seamless power during load shedding.
•	Fibre internet and DSTV dual-view installed.
Outdoor & Garden Features
•	Established, low-maintenance garden with 9 fruit-bearing olive trees.
•	Automated irrigation system and well-designed rainwater drainage.
•	Fully paved back yard and driveway for durability and ease of maintenance.
Thoughtful Extras
•	Hidden reverse-osmosis water filtration system in the kitchen.
•	High-quality furniture and finishes throughout - a significant investment has been  made into upgrades.

A Move-In Ready Home with Exceptional Value
Set in a convenient, central location with mountain views, this property is ideal for buyers seeking modern security, comfort, and style without the hassle of renovating an old property or furnishing a home. With every detail considered - from air-cons to fibre to full furnishings - this home truly offers a painless transition into coastal living.
Come and view before it's too late.

Come and make an offer on all furniture inside house.
Priced to be sold. Please Call for appointment.

descriptionHeader: Spacious Home with Flatlet in Prime Location
description:
This property offers well-proportioned accommodation and a layout suited to family living or extended use.

The main house comprises three spacious bedrooms, all fitted with built-in cupboards and high ceilings, as well as a study that can be used as a fourth bedroom. The main bedroom includes an en-suite bathroom, with a second bathroom servicing the remainder of the home.

Living areas are generous and practical. A large living room with built-in braai opens onto a lawned garden, providing a direct indoor-outdoor connection. The kitchen is well sized, with an open-plan dining area leading to a small patio area for everyday use.

The entrance to the home is well defined, with a circular driveway and an established front garden featuring mature agapanthus, contributing to a neat and welcoming street presence.

Additional features include wooden flooring throughout, a separate laundry room, and a storeroom (previously utilised as a garage, no vehicular access). A separate bachelor flat, requiring some attention, includes its own private courtyard and offers flexibility for rental income, guests, or independent accommodation.

There is ample on-site parking, and the overall layout is efficient and functional, with no unnecessary or wasted space.

A beautiful property offers space, flexibility, and long-term practicality in an established central residential area close to Bredasdorp Primary School.

descriptionHeader: Spacious Family Home in Quiet Swellendam Street – Close to School & Nature Trails
description:
Situated in a quiet area of Swellendam, this spacious family home offers comfort, charm, and convenience. Within walking distance of the high school, as well as popular hiking and cycling trails, this property is ideal for a family that enjoys an active outdoor lifestyle. The home features 3 spacious bedrooms, two of which open onto a balcony/stoep, and 2 bathrooms, including a main en suite. The living areas consist of a family room, lounge, and dining room with a cozy fireplace, perfect for winter evenings. A welcoming double front door leads to generous interiors and a spacious kitchen with ample cupboards, a breakfast nook, and a separate laundry area. 

The tandem garage provides secure parking and extra storage space. The property is walled and fitted with security gates and burglar bars, offering peace of mind. The established garden is beautifully maintained and features lovely Iceberg roses, adding to the home’s charm and appeal. This well-located property combines modern comfort with the tranquil lifestyle Swellendam is known for — a wonderful opportunity for families looking to settle in this sought-after Western Cape town.

Swellendam, located at the foot of the majestic Langeberg Mountains in the Western Cape, is one of South Africa’s most charming and historic towns. Known for its beautiful Cape Dutch architecture, tree-lined streets, and welcoming community, Swellendam offers a relaxed country lifestyle with modern conveniences. The town boasts excellent schools, medical facilities, and a variety of restaurants, coffee shops, and art galleries. Outdoor enthusiasts enjoy the nearby Marloth Nature Reserve, hiking and cycling trails, and easy access to surrounding wine and fruit farms.
Ideally situated between Cape Town and the Garden Route, Swellendam provides the perfect balance of small-town tranquility and accessibility. Just 220 km from Cape Town, Swellendam offers a peaceful escape with the conveniences of modern living.

descriptionHeader: Prime 1141 sqm Commercial Land in Kleinmond Central
description:
Discover an exceptional commercial development opportunity in the scenic Kleinmond, offering a substantial 1141 sqm. This prime vacant land parcel presents a blank canvas for investors and developers seeking to establish a significant presence in a growing coastal region. With its strategic location and generous size, the property is ideally suited for a diverse range of commercial ventures, from retail outlets and office spaces to mixed-use developments, catering to the needs of both local residents and the burgeoning tourism market.

The property boasts excellent road frontage and high visibility, ensuring maximum exposure for any future enterprise. Its proximity to established commercial amenities, including the Erika Centrum and various local shops, underscores its potential as a vibrant commercial hub. The surrounding infrastructure is well-developed, providing essential services and convenient access for customers and employees alike. This presents a unique chance to design and construct a bespoke commercial building tailored to specific business requirements, capitalising on the area's economic growth.

Investing in this Kleinmond commercial land offers significant development upside. The flexibility to create a purpose-built facility allows for optimal space utilisation and modern design, attracting quality tenants or serving as a flagship location for your own business. The area's natural beauty, characterised by majestic mountain views and coastal charm, adds an appealing backdrop, enhancing the overall desirability of the location for commercial operations.

Kleinmond, a picturesque town in the Overberg region of South Africa, is renowned for its coastal beach access, aquatic activities, and proximity to the Winelands, all set against a stunning mountain landscape. This vibrant community attracts both permanent residents and holidaymakers, ensuring a consistent flow of potential clientele for businesses established here.
The owner is registered for VAT and the price quoted is excluding VAT.

Key Features:
*   1141 sqm Commercial Erf
*   Prime Development Opportunity
*   Excellent Road Frontage andamp; Visibility
*   Proximity to Commercial Amenities
*   Scenic Mountain Views
*   Coastal andamp; Winelands Region
*   Versatile Commercial Potential
*   Established Infrastructure Access

descriptionHeader: Very well-built and neat family home located in Betty's Bay
description: This is a rare opportunity to still find a property in Betty's Bay for under R3 million. This warm and friendly 3 bedroom, 2 bathroom, (one en suite) house is for sale in our beautiful fynbos town. The main bedroom also has a walk-in closet. Entering the house from an undercover stoep, to the left is a spacious open plan living and dining area, equipped with a fire place. This area is also open plan to the kitchen with a gas stove. Leading from the kitchen is a pantry and a scullery/laundry with a back door to the braai stoep. A wonderful feature is the enclosed braai and entertainment area leading from the dining area. A single garage has a door connected to the house and the garage door is equipped with a remote control door. The plot is fully fenced with a paved drive way and a very neat garden.

descriptionHeader: Tranquil Living with Panoramic Mountain Views
description:
EXCLUSIVE MANDATE:
This spacious, north-facing one-bedroom unit offers uninterrupted views of the mountains and countryside. The open-plan living area flows seamlessly onto an enclosed patio with sliding doors that open onto a wooden deck — the perfect spot to relax and enjoy the beautiful mountain views.

The generously sized bedroom features built-in cupboards, while the full bathroom includes a modern walk-in shower. The unit is fully tiled and exceptionally well-maintained. The single garage offers direct access, includes a laundry area, and leads to a private, enclosed undercover courtyard.

Residents benefit from on-site frail care and dining facilities, with levies covering exterior maintenance, building insurance, and garden services.

Contact us today to arrange a viewing.

descriptionHeader: Large Plot near Struisbaai Swimming Beach
description:
Dual Mandate - This magnificently positioned plot is located a few metres from the boardwalk connecting Argonauta Park to the main swimming beach. The plot was sub-divided from the iconic "Sandkasteel" home. 

One of the added benefits is definitely the established trees which are a big bonus if you are planning to build your home. The plot currently hosts an older boat garage which could be removed or salvaged, should you wish to make use of the structure. 

This plot is a rare availability on the Struisbaai coast, as it even offers high walls for added security. The 810sqm size is one of the highlights as you would be able to build your favoured design without being limited in terms of lay-out and size. Well planned homes might have some sea view from the top level. 

Contact us to pursue the road to real estate happiness, and the prospects of a lucrative, growing investment.
